item.title: 国土安全部停摆致使关于联邦应急管理局的重大报告悬而未决
item.description: 据该报告的撰写者之一透露，美国国土安全部的停摆使一份关于如何改革国家顶级灾难机构的报告陷入悬而未决的境地。这份报告的出台已久经等待。【原文：The shutdown of the US Department of Homeland Security has thrust into limbo a long-awaited report on how to revamp the nation’s top disaster agency, according to one of the report authors.】

item.title: 基因改造香蕉初创企业Tropic获1.05亿美元融资以提升产量
item.description: 英国基因编辑初创企业Tropic Biosciences UK Ltd.已完成1.05亿美元新一轮融资，用于扩大生产不易褐变的香蕉。【原文：Tropic Biosciences UK Ltd., a British gene-editing startup, has raised $105 million in new financing to expand production of bananas that don’t brown.】

item.title: 热浪打破开普敦三月气温纪录
item.description: 开普敦及南非西开普省其他地区3月气温创历史新高，热浪肆虐的该地区易受干旱和野火双重威胁。【原文：Cape Town and other areas of South Africa’s Western Cape Province saw record temperatures for March as a heat wave sears a region that’s vulnerable to both drought and wildfires.】

item.title: 美国气象预报员称，导致全球变暖的厄尔尼诺现象将于九月形成
item.description: 美国气象预报员表示，太平洋海域很可能在9月前出现厄尔尼诺现象，这将导致全球气温上升，并可能在未来数月内影响农作物生长。【原文：US forecasters say an El Niño is favored to emerge in the Pacific Ocean by September, threatening to drive global temperatures higher and disrupt crops in the months ahead.】

item.title: 战争如何加剧伊朗水危机
item.description: 此外：绿色水泥初创企业Sublime Systems裁员三分之二。【原文：Also: Green cement startup Sublime Systems lays off two-thirds of its workers.】

item.title: 印度转向高污染燃料应对严峻供应短缺
item.description: 印度正转向生物质、煤油和燃料油等污染性燃料，因战争引发的天然气短缺正给该国各家各户和工业部门带来沉重负担。【原文：India is turning to polluting fuels such as biomass, kerosene and fuel oil as war-led gas shortages weigh on households and industries across the country.】

item.title: 战争正将伊朗供水系统推向崩溃边缘
item.description:
气候变化引发的干旱与管理不善已使该国陷入严重缺水困境，而战争更使水资源压力雪上加霜。【原文：

Climate-fueled drought and mismanagement have left the country with a severe water shortage, and war only adds to the strain. 

】

item.title: 特斯拉能源部门获英国供电许可
item.description: 特斯拉公司旗下能源部门已获准在英国供电，这使该公司能够接触到英国家庭和企业的潜在客户。【原文：Tesla Inc.’s energy division has been granted a license to supply electricity in the UK, giving the company access to potential customers in British homes and businesses.】

item.title: 《所见之处皆是气候变迁的创伤》：艾比·达雷谈创作气候小说
item.description:
畅销小说家兼气候小说奖得主做客《零》节目，探讨为何在讲述尼日利亚女孩故事时无法忽视气候变化问题。

【原文：

Bestselling novelist and winner of the Climate Fiction Prize joins Zero to discuss why she couldn’t ignore climate change as she told the story of girls in Nigeria.

】

item.title: 绿色水泥初创企业因特朗普削减支持而大幅裁员
item.description:
总部位于马萨诸塞州的Sublime Systems公司已裁减三分之二的现有员工，这令其向微软供应低碳材料的交易前景蒙上阴影。

【原文：

Massachusetts-based Sublime Systems has cut two-thirds of its current workforce, casting doubt on a deal to supply Microsoft with low-carbon material.

】

item.title: 房地产基金面临"终极风险"，能源成本飙升
item.description: 油价和天然气价格的飙升，让商业地产投资者若不降低建筑对传统能源的依赖，所面临的风险暴露无遗。【原文：Soaring oil and gas prices have thrust into plain sight the risk investors in commercial real estate face if they don’t make their buildings less reliant on old-world energy.】

item.title: 远见集团将收购新西兰清洁能源公司并投资3.6亿美元
item.description: 总部位于伦敦的投资管理公司Foresight Group Holdings Ltd.的澳大利亚分支机构将收购新西兰可再生能源开发商NZ Clean Energy，计划在未来十二个月内投资5亿澳元（约合3.6亿美元）。【原文：The Australian arm of London-based investment manager Foresight Group Holdings Ltd. will acquire New Zealand renewables developer NZ Clean Energy, with plans to invest A$500 million ($360 million) over the next twelve months.】

item.title: 创纪录高温将炙烤洛杉矶及美国西南部地区
item.description: 高温将削减加利福尼亚州的积雪量，从而增加干旱卷土重来的风险。【原文：The high temperatures will cut into California’s snowpack, raising the risk that drought will return.】

item.title: 创纪录的三月高温将炙烤洛杉矶及美国西南部
item.description: 一场异常早的热浪即将席卷洛杉矶及美国西南部大部分地区，这将危及加州本已脆弱的积雪层，进而威胁该州的水资源供应。【原文：An unusually early heat wave is set to grip Los Angeles and much of the Southwest, putting California’s already fragile snowpack, and therefore its water supplies, at risk.】

item.title: 肯尼亚计划在蒙巴萨港附近建设29亿美元天然气发电厂
item.description: 肯尼亚计划在其东海岸建造一座1200兆瓦的燃气发电厂，以扩大电力产量满足不断增长的需求。【原文：Kenya plans to build a 1,200-megawatt gas-fired power plant on its east coast as it ramps up electricity output to meet growing demand.】

item.title: 尽管推动清洁能源，天然气仍主导英国发电容量拍卖
item.description: 在英国最近的市场拍卖中，燃气发电厂获得了近60%的发电容量，这凸显了它们在能源系统中的核心地位——即便在该国大力推动清洁能源发展的背景下亦是如此。【原文：Gas-fired plants secured almost 60% of capacity in the UK’s latest market auction, underscoring their central role in the energy system even as the country pushes for cleaner power.】

item.title: 英国气候变化委员会称：实现净零排放的成本低于一次化石燃料价格冲击
item.description: 英国官方气候顾问表示，实现净零排放的成本低于单次化石燃料价格冲击。【原文：The cost of meeting net zero is less than a single fossil fuel price shock, the UK’s official climate adviser said.】

item.title: 气候变化正使极端高温变得难以忍受
item.description: 全球越来越多的地区正面临致命天气。【原文：The weather is becoming deadly in a growing number of locations around the world.】

item.title: 极端高温正使生活日益难以忍受
item.description:
一项新研究发现，自1950年以来，因天气过热而无法进行日常活动的小时数已翻倍。【原文：

The number of hours where it’s too hot for everyday activities has doubled since 1950, a new study found.

】

item.title: 冯德莱恩称欧盟逐步淘汰核能是"错误之举"
item.description: 欧盟委员会主席乌尔苏拉·冯德莱恩表示，欧盟放弃核能是"战略性错误"，此番言论是对德国关闭核反应堆决定的委婉批评。【原文：The European Union made a “strategic mistake” in turning its back on nuclear energy, Commission President Ursula von der Leyen said in thinly-veiled criticism of Germany’s decision to close its reactors.】

item.title: 20亿英镑债券基金因核风险回避绿色国债发售
item.description: 英国决定将即将发行的绿色国债所得用于核能项目，这一决定已成为Rathbones资产管理公司旗舰债券基金的交易终结者。【原文：The UK’s decision to use the proceeds of its upcoming green gilt sale on nuclear energy has emerged as a dealbreaker for Rathbones Asset Management’s flagship bond fund.】

item.title: 杰富瑞力推加倍投资清洁技术领域
item.description: 为何银行建议客户坚持投资可再生能源，尽管面临供应链和通胀风险。【原文：Why the bank is telling clients to stick with renewables despite supply chain and inflation risks.】

item.title: 肯尼亚首都受灾最重 洪水已造成至少37人死亡
item.description: 肯尼亚多地周五晚间遭遇暴雨，引发洪水，造成至少37人死亡。【原文：At least 37 people died in flooding after heavy rains in several parts of Kenya on Friday night.】

item.title: 欧盟将推出基础设施基金推动绿色能源转型
item.description: 欧盟将启动一项新的投资基金，以助力实现未来15年内该集团绿色能源转型所需的数万亿欧元支出。【原文：The European Union will launch a new investment fund to help deliver the trillions of euros of spending that will be needed over the next 15 years for the bloc’s green-energy transition.】

item.title: 中国持续推进核电发展，设定雄心勃勃的增长目标
item.description: 尽管近年来屡屡受挫，中国仍通过设定雄心勃勃的新目标，持续向核电巨头施压以交付更多反应堆。【原文：China is maintaining the pressure on its nuclear power giants to deliver more reactors with an ambitious new target, despite a string of misses in recent years.】

item.title: 日本福岛核事故清理工作为核灾后重建提供范本
item.description:
2011年遭受重创的核电站取得突破性进展，正在测试一种关闭核反应堆的新方法。

【原文：

A breakthrough at the plant devastated in 2011 is testing a new approach to shutting down atomic reactors.

】

item.title: 德黑兰遭袭击后陷入火海、浓烟与酸雨之中
item.description: 德黑兰居民清晨醒来，发现城市天际线笼罩在黑烟与酸雨云中——以色列空袭点燃了这座中东最大城市之一的关键燃料库。【原文：Tehran’s residents woke up to a skyline shrouded in black smoke and acid rain clouds after Israeli airstrikes set ablaze key fuel depots serving one of the Middle East’s largest cities.】

item.title: 伊朗战争考验清洁技术行业是否吸取了2022年的教训
item.description: 对绿色投资者而言，当前因战争推高的油气价格令人想起2022年的痛苦记忆——当时可再生能源遭遇抛售潮，且持续至2025年。但杰富瑞公司正敦促客户加倍押注于大胆乐观的预测。【原文：For green investors, the current war-fueled surge in oil and gas prices conjures up painful memories of 2022, when renewables were gripped by a selloff that lasted into 2025. But at Jefferies, clients are being urged to double down on a bold upbeat call.】

item.title: 英国银行遭洪水再保险公司首席执行官抨击，因其"忽视"抵押贷款风险
item.description: 英国最后防线的洪水保险机构表示，该国抵押贷款银行在应对全英房主面临的风险方面准备不足。【原文：The UK’s flood insurer of last resort says the country’s mortgage banks aren’t doing enough to prepare for the risks facing homeowners across Britain.】

item.title: 普拉格电力计划在美国顶级电网拍卖中推出氢能项目
item.description: 普拉格电力公司计划在美国最大电网即将举行的特别拍卖中提供氢能电力，以满足人工智能热潮带来的能源需求。【原文：Plug Power Inc. is planning to offer hydrogen electricity in a potential special auction by the biggest US power grid in the scramble to feed the artificial intelligence boom.】
